name: FTC Rule on the Use of Consumer Reviews and Testimonials (Fake Reviews Rule)
summary: Federal rule prohibiting fake reviews, review suppression, and undisclosed insider reviews.

evidence.claim: FTC finalized the Fake Reviews Rule on August 14, 2024, effective October 21, 2024.

The rule specifically prohibits: (1) AI-generated fake reviews, (2) buying positive or negative reviews, (3) insider reviews without disclosure, (4) review suppression via legal threats or intimidation, (5) company-controlled review websites appearing independent, (6) fake indicators of social media influence.

body.officialName: Rule on the Use of Consumer Reviews and Testimonials
body.scopeSummary: Prohibits: AI-generated fake reviews; buying positive or negative reviews; insider reviews without disclosure; review suppression via legal threats; company-controlled review websites presenting as independent; fake indicators of social media influence (E2).
body.userRightsPlainLanguage: Fake reviews and testimonials are now illegal under federal US law. If you see a product with reviews that seem fake — bursts of identical phrasing, all five stars, all posted within a narrow window — and you believe they're violating this rule, you can report them to the FTC.
